steps.title: Clear the fractions
steps.content:
Multiply the equation by $80cx$ to remove denominators.
$$80=cx^2+80x$$
$$cx^2+80x-80=0$$

steps.title: Use one solution
steps.content:
A quadratic has exactly one solution when its discriminant is $0$. For $cx^2+80x-80=0$, use $a=c$, $b=80$, and $c_0=-80$ to avoid confusing the coefficient name with the constant term.
$$80^2-4(c)(-80)=0$$

steps.title: Solve the discriminant
steps.content:
Simplify and isolate the constant.
$$6{,}400+320c=0$$
$$320c=-6{,}400$$
$$c=-20$$
The answer is -20.
